candidate_mechanism.title: Spatialized Plasticity Coding Equation

equations.spatial_gate: q_t = sigmoid(alpha * (r_t - s_t) + beta * b_region - gamma * C_local)
equations.feature_field: f_{t+1}(i) = (1 - l_f) * f_t(i) + e_f * q_t(i) * [L_t(i) + k_a * sum_j A_t(i,j)L_t(j) - k_i I_t(i)]
equations.spatial_efficiency: E_3d = T_eff / (lambda_w C_wire + lambda_d C_delay + lambda_i I_global)
equations.coding_state: y_t = W_f f_t + W_A vec(A_t) + W_m m_t

interpretation.spatial_gate: local physical crowding and route demand jointly decide whether a region should keep growing features or consolidate structure
interpretation.feature_field: feature extraction is a routed field update over a 3D substrate, not a coordinate-free vector lookup
interpretation.structure_field: effective topology only spends long-range cost when coactivation and demand exceed prune and spatial penalties
interpretation.memory_field: slow memory stores durable long-range scaffolds and suppresses unstable geometric overgrowth
interpretation.wire_cost: 3D distance is a real penalty, but bundled local neighborhoods discount that penalty for efficient tracts
interpretation.spatial_efficiency: efficiency is information throughput per unit physical wiring, delay, and interference cost

candidate_mechanism.core_claim: The brain is efficient in 3D not because geometry directly encodes meaning, but because a 3D wiring substrate lets local neighborhoods stay dense and cheap while plasticity continuously builds a sparse effective topology for high-value long-range integration.

project_readout.summary: P8A is positive only if the project can write one spatialized plasticity-coding equation that fits local topology reuse, compact relation bridges, and the failure of geometry-only baselines.
project_readout.next_question: If P8A holds, the next step is to explain in more detail how 3D wiring economy and dynamic topology divide labor, and then derive new spatial falsifiers.
